title: vercel-labs/deepsec: coding agents that probe a codebase for vulnerabilities
friendlyTitle: Deepsec uses coding agents to look for security problems
summary: Deepsec is a roughly 6,000-star security harness that uses coding agents to investigate vulnerabilities in a codebase. Recent work includes configurable reasoning levels and a fix for unbounded memory growth during sandbox downloads.
friendlySummary: Deepsec sends AI coding agents through a codebase to search for possible weaknesses. The open-source project has about 6,000 GitHub stars.
whyItMatters: Development teams can use it as an additional adversarial pass to explore attack paths before release, while keeping findings inside a repeatable harness. Agent-discovered issues still require reproduction and human security review; this is a testing layer, not a replacement for secure design or professional auditing.
friendlyWhyItMatters: It can add another security check before release. A security expert should still confirm every finding.
